sessions.notes: s32 idx2 = D_800A3748; declared first in block before *(entry+0x5E)=1 store, then call. Retires the reorder 36,38,40,39,37,41 regfix rule. Insn count 60->58 matches target's 59 +/-1.

NEW MECHANISM FOUND: `s32 a1_s = (s16)a1;` as a separate top-level local (used in `(u32)(a1_s - 0x17) >= 2`) DOES trigger target's andi materialization. Build_insns 58->59 = target exactly. Confirmed via objdump: andi $v1,$a1,0xFFFF appears at idx 18, addiu uses $a1, lhu lands in $a1 (all matching target's register pattern). HOWEVER: (a) the (s16) cast compiles to `lh` (sign-ext load) not target's `lhu` (zero-ext) — load opcode mismatch is 1 byte; (b) the additional local inflates stack frame by 8 bytes (addiu sp,-40 vs target -32; 3 saved-reg offsets shift +8). Net score 9 (was 3), but the BREAKTHROUGH is finding what produces the andi. Next session must keep the (s16)-cast effect while keeping lhu + avoiding the stack inflation. Forms tested negative this session: (s16)a1 inline in expression (no separate local) -> +2 insns sll/sra emission; (u32)a1 cast on comparison -> score 3 unchanged; (s32)a1 - 0x17 precomputed as local -> score 5; re-read pattern *(u16*)(entry+0x6A) -> CSE folded.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — confirmed reproducibility of floor 3, explored 1 untried form
sessions.notes: Resumed from candidate.c (floor 3 confirmed: score=3, build_insns=58, target=59). Tried one form from next_hypotheses (ii) — inline *(s16 *)(entry + 0x6A) re-read at the comparison position (instead of the cached u16 a1): produced score 3 but build_insns=61 (+2 over target). This is the same +2 sll/sra emission penalty as the inline (s16)a1 rejected_form (the s16 pointer dereference compiles to sign-extend RTL that emits two extra shifts when not folded into a local). The lhu→lh load conversion still happens at the explicit s16 read. No improvement, no new lever found. Other candidate forms considered but not measured: nested struct/union punning (cheat-by-spelling concern — no semantic purpose), opaque mask variable (already documented as cheats-by-spelling violation in notes.md), DImode chain (forbidden per [[global-label-drift-sibling-cheat]]). Per session-2 analysis the (s16)-cast-on-local mechanism is the ONLY form found that triggers andi emission, and its two coupled regressions (lh load + stack +8) are structural — defeating either requires a C-source path that produces the SImode sign-extension RTL at the COMPARISON site without propagating back to the load (lhu) and without forcing a new spill slot. No such path identified this session.

reviewer.evidence: Not invoked. Lever applied is the documented [[hoist-call-arg-local-flips-jal-delay]] pattern with confirmed sanctioned precedent (func_80060B70 / commit 6a0a83d0). idx2 has semantic content (call's arg0). Recommend reviewer invocation if/when candidate.c is applied to src/ at the score-3 floor.

remaining_gap.summary: 3 diffs in comparison block at maspsx idx 14-22: lhu lands in $v1 (target $a1); missing 'andi $v1, $a1, 0xFFFF' insn at idx 16; addiu in bnez delay slot uses $v1 (target $a1). The 3 are coupled: forcing GCC to materialize the andi would naturally allocate $v1 to the andi result and push lhu into $a1.
rules_in_remaining_cluster: func_8001EEB4: $3 <-> $5 @ 21-21 (addiu in delay slot reg swap)

rejected_forms.reason: Combine folds the redundant mask; no andi emitted. Same score as baseline.
GCC combine collapses lh+cast back to lhu equivalent. No andi materialization.
register keyword is a hint GCC ignores. No allocation change.
GCC compiles && and goto-end forms identically. No codegen change. Note: per [[goto-end-prologue-delay-slot]] using accumulator+shared-end purely for codegen would be a cheat anyway.

rejected_forms.form: s8 idx2 = D_800A3748; (not s32) as hoisted second-call arg
rejected_forms.reason: s8 type triggers lbu+sll24+sra24 expansion (extra 2 insns). Use s32 instead — lb directly without sign-ext shifts.
Hoists the subtract out of the && chain, regressing the scheduling. Insn count drops to 57 (target 59).
GCC sees u16 -> u32 promotion as no-op (lhu already zero-extends). No codegen change.
CSE folds back to the cached value in the local. No second lhu emitted.

build_insns 61 vs target 59. The inline (s16) emits sll 16 + sra 16 to sign-extend the u16-loaded value (+2 insns regression). The separate-local form (see sessions[2]) is the lever direction; inline doesn't trigger andi the same way.

rejected_forms.form: nested-if with block-scoped s32 a1_s = (s16)a1 inside inner if
rejected_forms.reason: build_insns 61 vs target 59. Same lhu kept (good — doesn't force the load to lh) but combine processes the sign-extension in the inner block's RTL after the outer if's RTL has already settled, so the andi DOESN'T materialize. Block-scope kills the lever's effect. Function-scope local is REQUIRED for the andi trigger.

Identical asm output to s32 a1_s = (s16)a1. (s32)(s16) double-cast and (s16) single-cast produce the same RTL — the inner (s16) is what matters; the outer (s32) is redundant on a value already widening to int via default promotion.

rejected_forms.form: inline *(s16 *)(entry + 0x6A) re-read at comparison (kept u16 a1 for first test, re-read s16 in 3rd test slot)
rejected_forms.reason: build_insns 61 vs target 59. Same +2 sll/sra penalty as inline (s16)a1 cast. The s16 pointer dereference at the comparison position compiles to a sign-extended load that emits two shift insns when not stored to a local first. The hoped-for separation between load (lhu, cached in a1) and sign-extension (at the 3rd test) doesn't help — the load opcode at the inline s16 read site is its own lh, and combine doesn't materialize an andi here either.

next_hypotheses: **PRIMARY (session-2 finding)**: The `s32 a1_s = (s16)a1;` separate top-level local DOES trigger the andi materialization (build_insns 58->59 = target, andi/$a1/$v1 register pattern all match). Two coupled regressions to solve: (a) the (s16) cast forces the LOAD itself to `lh` instead of target's `lhu` (sign-extend at the memory read, not just on the cached value); (b) the local inflates the stack frame by 8 bytes. Resume by finding a form that produces the sign-extension RTL effect (which is what triggers the andi) WITHOUT changing the load opcode AND without forcing a stack spill. Candidates: (i) block-local declaration inside the if-arm to limit live range; (ii) read the value separately as both u16 AND s16 via *(u16*)/*(s16*) at different syntactic positions; (iii) routing through (s32)(s16)a1 vs (s16)(s32)a1 different cast orders.

Find a source pattern that defeats combine's (and (mem:HI) 0xFFFF) -> (mem:HI) fold WITHOUT triggering the lh-load conversion. Per session-2: the (s16) cast is the only form found so far that disrupts the fold, but it does so by changing the LOAD's mode. A purely RTL-level approach: find a C construct that keeps a SImode pseudo distinct from the HImode load throughout combine.

Read GCC 2.7.2 tools/gcc-2.7.2/combine.c for the specific simplify rule folding the andi here. Use instrumented cc1 (tmp/gccdbg/ with BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG / SCHED_DEBUG / PRIO_DEBUG hooks) to dump combine input/output for the lhu region. Per [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] this is the load-bearing diagnostic.

Cross-reference sibling functions with the same lhu/andi/beq pattern in target: func_8001A820 (empty body, no source clue), func_8001F938 (INCOMPLETE, score 11, 13 rules), calc_loc_mat_fw_80055B60 (ASM-STRUCTURAL park bucket). None have matched yet — solving one may unlock the others (or document the family's irreducibility).

Permuter from the candidate-floor-3 base. Set up permuter/func_8001EEB4/ with candidate.c as base, run with --stop-on-zero. Per the project's permuter discipline use clean single-function target.s + prelude.inc (drop .set gp=64). With the session-2 finding (the (s16)-cast-local form), seed permuter from that floor-9 base too — permuter may find the structural tweak that fixes the lh and stack regressions.
